articles.title: GBP/JPY extends rally to 2008 highs as elevated Oil prices weigh on the Yen
articles.expand: GBP/JPY advances on Tuesday, extending gains for a seventh consecutive day as elevated Oil prices continue to weigh on the Japanese Yen (JPY), while improving market sentiment, driven by renewed hopes of US-Iran negotiations, supports the British Pound (GBP).

articles.title: Dollar Selloff Intensifies as Soft PPI Eases Fed Pressure, US-Iran Optimism Builds
articles.expand: Dollar is being hit from both sides—soft inflation and warmer diplomacy. A cooler-than-expected PPI reading has eased pressure on the Federal Reserve to turn more hawkish, while renewed optimism around US-Iran negotiations is unwinding the war premium that briefly supported the greenback. Together, these forces are driving an extended, broad-based ...

articles.title: GBP/USD Finds Support: Geopolitics Already Priced In, Focus on Bank of England
articles.expand: By Analytical Department RoboForex GBP/USD rose to 1.3506 on Tuesday. Sterling has moved comfortably away from last week’s one-month high of 1.3480. Pressure on the currency had previously increased following the collapse of US-Iran talks over the weekend. The breakdown in dialogue followed Tehran’s refusal to abandon its nuclear progra...

articles.title: Gulf region: Shock resilience and investment outlook – BNP Paribas
articles.expand: BNP Paribas’ Stéphane ALBY assesses how Gulf economies are absorbing the conflict-related shock. He notes that Oil exports via the Strait of Hormuz have been severely disrupted, with Bahrain, Kuwait, and Qatar hit hardest, while Saudi Arabia and the UAE partially benefit from higher Oil prices.

articles.title: TRY: Faster Lira depreciation risk after heavy FX use – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Tatha Ghose warns that USD/TRY is gaining momentum towards 45.00 as Turkey faces a worsening external backdrop and extended energy price shock.

articles.title: EUR/USD Price Forecast: Recovering 1.1800, what’s next?
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair trades at around 1.1800 for the first time since last February, as optimism heavily weighs on the US Dollar (USD) demand.

articles.title: RBA: Higher rates risk with inflation pressures – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities analysts highlight a hawkish shift from the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) after Deputy Governor Hauser signaled limited confidence that current policy is sufficient to return inflation to target.

articles.title: ECB: Hawkish arguments build on new analysis – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Christoph Rieger flags that European Central Bank (ECB) communication and research around the IMF Spring meetings could support a more forceful policy stance.

articles.title: AUD/USD rallies beyond 0.7100 amid hopes of new US-Iran peace talks
articles.expand: The Australian Dollar (AUD) appreciated for the second consecutive day against the US Dollar (USD) on Tuesday, reaching four-week highs above 0.7120 at the time of writing and drawing closer to the 2026 high at 0.7187.

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP/USD seems poised to climb further
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair is seen building on the previous day's strong move up of around 125-pips and gaining some follow-through traction on Tuesday.

articles.title: EUR: Hormuz inflation shock and EU politics – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank analysts flag that final March CPI across the Eurozone will refine how the Hormuz-driven energy shock feeds into Euro-area inflation.

articles.title: NZD/USD Price Forecast: Extends rally to near 0.5900 after recovering from 20-day EMA
articles.expand: The NZD/USD pair trades 0.5% higher to near 0.5900 during the European trading session on Tuesday. The Kiwi pair strengthens as the New Zealand Dollar (NZD) outperforms its peers amid risk-on market sentiment.

articles.title: Optimism Weighs on the Greenback
articles.expand: North American participants shrugged off the pessimism seen in Asia Pacific and Europe yesterday after the US-Iran negotiations broke down over the weekend. The optimism was contagious and reports that another round of negotiations are being contemplated during the ceasefire that expires next week. The dollar is broadly weaker. Global equities and ...

articles.title: EUR/GBP: BoE caution supports cross – ING
articles.expand: ING strategist Francesco Pesole notes that Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey and hawkish MPC members Catherine Mann and Megan Greene speak today, with Greene recently sounding more balanced.

articles.title: EUR/USD: Resilience near pre-war highs – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale strategists note EUR/USD has squeezed through 1.1750 and is approaching its pre-conflict level of 1.18, with support at 1.1673 and resistance at 1.1830.

articles.title: GBP/USD Price Forecast: Seems poised to climb further as USD weakens on US-Iran optimism
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair is seen building on the previous day's strong move up of around 125-pips and gaining some follow-through traction on Tuesday.

articles.title: JPY: April hike risk and policy lag – OCBC
articles.expand: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong highlight that Japanese Yen underperformed despite a softer US Dollar, as muted BoJ communication left it isolated. Markets have pared April BoJ hike expectations, yet OCBC still sees an April rate hike as likely.

articles.expand: This is mostly a repeat to what we've heard from earlier in the day, that both sides are eyeing talks on Thursday in Islamabad. But as the echo chamber gets louder, we're seeing market players pick up on the optimism and running with it. It's a funny thing that even though the latest development is essentially a reset to last week, markets are grow...

articles.expand: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, we don't have much on the agenda other than the final Spanish CPI. Given that it's the final data and that the market focus remains solely on the US-Iran negotiations, the market reaction will likely be muted. articles.title: RBA’s Hauser Warns of ‘Central Banker’s Nightmare’ as Oil Shock Lifts Inflation, Hits Growth
articles.expand: The RBA is facing a “central banker’s nightmare” as rising oil prices push inflation higher while threatening economic activity. Speaking in New York, Deputy Governor Andrew Hauser warned that the Middle East conflict is delivering a significant income shock to Australia, complicating the policy outlook at a time when inflation is already “too high...

articles.expand: Australian consumer sentiment has collapsed back to crisis-era levels as fuel prices and rising interest rates bite. but the RBA is still expected to press ahead with rate hikes as inflation remains the dominant concern. articles.expand: CHF/JPY has been on a steady climb these days, but its rally might be cut short as it closes in on a long-term ceiling. Will we see a bounce or break from here?

articles.title: PBOC sets USD/ CNY central rate at 6.8593 (vs. estimate at 6.8173)
articles.expand: The PBOC allows the yuan to fluctuate within a +/- 2% range, around this reference rate. PBOC injects 1bn yuan via 7-day reverse repos in open market operates today. Unchanged rate of 1.4%. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.

articles.title: AUD/USD Technical Analysis: Bulls regain control as key psychological level holds
articles.expand: AUD/USD: Technical analysis reveals a decidedly bullish outlook as bulls regain control. The pair successfully defended key support, broke a descending trendline (Daily), and confirmed a "Golden Cross" (H4). With the path of least resistance to the upside, the next major hurdle is 0.7130.

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Upside remains capped by 0.7100
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar's continuous rebound seems to have strengthened in the last few days, giving bulls back control and paving the way for AUD/USD to perhaps test the upper end of its annual range sooner rather than later.

articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Bulls seem hesitant as oil prices rally on failed US-Iran talks
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair attracts fresh sellers following an intraday move higher at the start of a new week and now seems to have stalled its recovery move from sub-1.3800 levels, or a nearly two-week low set on Friday.

articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: Middle East ceasefire hangs by a thread
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair came back to life this week, settling a handful of pips above the 1.1700 mark. Risk-on flows began late Tuesday, when United States (US) President Donald Trump withdrew his threat to destroy Iran.

articles.title: WTI Crude Oil: Traders Should Brace For Additional Spikes
articles.expand: WTI Crude Oil went into the this weekend above the 90.000 USD mark, and by now speculators should be ready in their thinking for the opening of trading tomorrow which is certain to be an adventure as the week starts.
